title: Winey Crispy Canadian Beef Dumplings
description: A light, low‑carb Canadian dish featuring wine-infused beef filling and crispy, golden dumplings.
instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 teaspoon of sesame oil, letting it shimmer for 30 seconds.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, sautéing until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the minced beef and cook until browned and crumbled, about 4 minutes.
Mix in the cauliflower rice and diced onion, cooking until the vegetables soften, about 3 minutes.
Pour in the soy sauce and white wine, seasoning with salt and pepper, and cook until the liquid has mostly evaporated, about 4 minutes.
Transfer the filling to a bowl and let it cool slightly before shaping the dumplings.
Place a spoonful of filling onto each low-carb wonton wrapper and fold over, sealing the edges with a touch of water.
Heat a small amount of oil in the same skillet over medium heat and arrange the dumplings in a single layer, skin side down.
Cook the dumplings until the bottom is golden-brown and crispy, about 3 minutes, then flip and cook the other side for 2 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with chopped green onions, and enjoy the wine-infused crispiness.
